app.title: WealthOS
app.tagline: The operating system for your household's financial strategy

next.title: What now?
next.mappingEmptyHead: Start here: add your first account
next.mappingEmptyDetail: Map a bank account, fund, or asset to build your wealth picture.
next.mappingItemsDetail: Move on to verifying the data before building strategy.
next.verifyReadyDetail: You can run the strategy engine now.
next.strategyRunDetail: Run the engine to get data-driven recommendations.
next.strategyClearDetail: You can move to ongoing monitoring.
next.allocationRunDetail: The engine orders every free shekel: buffer, expensive debt, tax ceilings, investing.
next.allocationPendingHead: A deployment plan awaits your approval
next.allocationDoneDetail: Advance to strategy via the verification page gate.

dashboard.noHousehold: Start by setting up your household.

mapping.emptyHint: Map your first item — for example your bank account — to build your wealth picture. You can add accounts, assets, mortgages, and cash flows.
mapping.title: Financial mapping
mapping.empty: Nothing mapped yet. Add your first item.
mapping.needMembers: Add family members before mapping items (ownership must be assigned).
mapping.suggestGrowthHint: Heuristic estimates from track names — flagged until you confirm.

earmark.title: Earmark to a goal
earmark.hint: Pin this account to a specific goal so the funding-gap math reflects how the family actually thinks.

fx.title: FX rates (manual)
fx.empty: No rates yet. Foreign-currency items are excluded from totals until a rate exists.
fx.refreshFromBoi: Fetch today's Bank of Israel rates
fx.refreshedBanner: Rates updated from Bank of Israel.

home.emptyState: The household map is empty. Milestone 1 adds manual mapping.

documents.title: Documents & import
documents.suspenseNote: Items the system refused to guess about. Classification UI arrives in the Verification milestone.

verification.explainer1: Verification is where you confirm every item reflects reality — a current valuation, its data source, and a confidence level. Unverified items block the move to strategy.
verification.explainer2: For each item you can confirm it ("correct as shown"), reject it with a note, or correct the value — the correction is appended to the valuation history and never overwrites it.
verification.explainer3: Suspense items (data the system couldn't attribute) must be resolved before this phase counts as complete.
verification.title: Verification
verification.gateOpen: All checks passed — you can advance to Strategy.
verification.gateBlocked: Blocked until every item is verified and suspense is empty.
verification.queueEmpty: Every item is verified. Nothing to review.

suspense.title: Resolve suspense item
suspense.createHint: Fields are prefilled from the raw data where possible. Review everything before saving.
suspense.resolveList: Open the pending list on the Documents page to pick items to resolve.

registry.title: Registry
registry.assumptionsHint: Conservative system defaults. Overrides create a new version and invalidate recommendations that depended on the old value.
registry.taxHint: Versioned, source-cited regulatory data. No rate exists anywhere in code.
registry.explainer1: The registry holds every ASSUMPTION the engines use (emergency-fund target, expected returns, your risk answers, drift thresholds) and the Israeli TAX RULES (brackets, hishtalmut/pension ceilings) — deliberately outside the code, so you can see and control them.
registry.explainer2: Overriding a value creates a new VERSION. Recommendations are pinned to the exact versions they were built with — changing an assumption marks affected recommendations as invalidated, so advice never silently rests on outdated inputs.
registry.explainer3: Tax rules carry their sources and stay marked "pending owner review" until you confirm the figures match reality.

expected_real_return_equity_pct.label: Expected real return — growth
expected_real_return_equity_pct.desc: Long-run annual return of the growth component (global equities) after inflation.

expected_real_return_bonds_pct.label: Expected real return — defensive
expected_real_return_bonds_pct.desc: Investment-grade bond return after inflation, long-run.

inflation_il_pct.label: Israeli inflation
inflation_il_pct.desc: Long-run price-growth assumption; BOI target is 1-3%.
inflation_il_pct.guide: 2-3% reasonable; mainly affects CPI-linked mortgage and scenarios.

goal_projection_real_return_pct.label: Return for goal projections
goal_projection_real_return_pct.desc: Real return used for funding gaps and income-derived targets.

mc_return_volatility_pct.label: Annual volatility (Monte Carlo)
mc_return_volatility_pct.desc: Standard deviation of growth returns in probabilistic simulations.

taxable_gain_fraction.label: Taxable gain fraction
taxable_gain_fraction.desc: Share of a taxable balance assumed to be gain when simulating withdrawals.

pension_withdrawal_effective_tax_pct.label: Effective pension withdrawal tax
pension_withdrawal_effective_tax_pct.desc: Estimated tax on pension-tier withdrawals in scenarios.

emergency_fund_months.label: Emergency fund (months)
emergency_fund_months.desc: Months of expenses required liquid before investing surpluses.

insurance_survivor_expense_months.label: Survivor cover (months)
insurance_survivor_expense_months.desc: Months of expenses life/survivor cover must provide before the insurance engine flags a gap.

low_confidence_threshold.label: Low-confidence threshold
low_confidence_threshold.desc: Items below this confidence score get flagged in verification.

staleness_days_by_kind.label: Valuation staleness by kind
staleness_days_by_kind.desc: Days after which an unrefreshed valuation counts as stale, per item kind.

strategy_min_completeness.label: Minimum data completeness
strategy_min_completeness.desc: The strategy engine refuses to run below this.

strategy_min_confidence.label: Minimum data confidence
strategy_min_confidence.desc: Overall confidence floor for strategy runs.

concentration_single_asset_max_pct.label: Max single-position share
concentration_single_asset_max_pct.desc: Above this share of assets one position is flagged.

concentration_institution_max_pct.label: Max single-institution share
concentration_institution_max_pct.desc: Above this share at one institution an operational notice appears.

currency_foreign_min_pct.label: Minimum foreign exposure
currency_foreign_min_pct.desc: Below this, a global-diversification recommendation appears.

currency_foreign_max_pct.label: Maximum foreign exposure
currency_foreign_max_pct.desc: Above this, a rebalance recommendation appears (expenses are in ILS).

management_fee_notice_pct.label: Fee notice threshold (global)
management_fee_notice_pct.desc: Balance fee above which fee-drag is flagged, when no per-type threshold exists.

management_fee_notice_by_type.label: Fee thresholds by product type
management_fee_notice_by_type.desc: Separate notice level per wrapper type.
management_fee_notice_by_type.guide: Calibrated to the 2026 Israeli market; update to your own best offers.

mortgage_cpi_linked_max_pct.label: Max CPI-linked mortgage share
mortgage_cpi_linked_max_pct.desc: Above this share of principal, a review recommendation appears.

expensive_debt_rate_pct.label: Expensive debt rate
expensive_debt_rate_pct.desc: Rate above which early repayment is suggested.

mortgage_prime_spread_pct.label: Prime spread
mortgage_prime_spread_pct.desc: Prime = BOI rate + this spread (1.5 in Israel).

mortgage_refinance_notice_spread_pct.label: Refinance notice spread
mortgage_refinance_notice_spread_pct.desc: Points above the live prime benchmark before a refinance recommendation.

large_loan_notice_base.label: Large loan review base
large_loan_notice_base.desc: Non-mortgage loans above this are flagged for review.

risk_loss_tolerance.label: Questionnaire: loss tolerance
risk_loss_tolerance.desc: Questionnaire answer (1-3). Prefer updating via the strategy-tab questionnaire.

allocation_rebalance_band_pct.label: Rebalance band (points)
allocation_rebalance_band_pct.desc: Allowed growth-share deviation from target before a recommendation.

allocation_real_estate_max_pct.label: Max real-estate share
allocation_real_estate_max_pct.desc: Above this share of gross assets a structural notice appears (never a sell instruction).

allocation_mix_unknown_max_pct.label: Unknown-mix ceiling
allocation_mix_unknown_max_pct.desc: If more of the investable value than this has unknown mix, the comparison is refused.
allocation_mix_unknown_max_pct.guide: Fix the data rather than raising this.

drift_allocation_pct.label: Allocation drift (monitoring)
drift_allocation_pct.desc: Growth-share movement since the strategy baseline that opens an alert.

drift_net_worth_pct.label: Net-worth drift (monitoring)
drift_net_worth_pct.desc: Relative net-worth change that opens an alert.

drift_liquidity_pct.label: Liquidity drift (monitoring)
drift_liquidity_pct.desc: Liquid-share change in points that opens an alert.

drift_concentration_pct.label: Concentration drift (monitoring)
drift_concentration_pct.desc: Top-position concentration increase that opens an alert.

drift_goal_funding_pct.label: Goal-coverage drift (monitoring)
drift_goal_funding_pct.desc: Goal-coverage change in points that opens an alert.

priority_weights.label: Recommendation priority weights
priority_weights.desc: How the priority score is computed (impact/ease/tax/risk/goals/urgency, sum 100).
priority_weights.guide: Raise the weight of what matters most to you.

risk_spending_flexibility.label: Questionnaire: spending flexibility
risk_spending_flexibility.desc: Ability to cut expenses in a bad stretch (1-3).

wizard.title: Plain-language setup wizard
wizard.cta: Answer a short plain-language questionnaire instead →
wizard.intro1: Fifteen short questions, no financial knowledge needed. Your answers set the alert thresholds and safety targets; market numbers (returns, inflation) stay expert-maintained.
wizard.intro2: Only values that actually change are written; each change creates a new version and invalidates recommendations built on the old one.
wizard.bufferMonths: If your income stopped tomorrow, how many months would you need to reorganize without selling investments?
wizard.spendRigidity: How much of your monthly spend is fixed (rent/mortgage, tuition)?
wizard.spendRigidity_1: Most of it — hard to cut
wizard.nagging: How much should the system nag you to refresh values?
wizard.nagging_1: A lot — I want everything current
wizard.concentrationSensitivity: How sensitive are you to one asset falling hard?
wizard.concentrationSensitivity_3: Not much — I can stomach it
wizard.israelDependence: How much of your economic future depends on Israel (career, property, family)?
wizard.driftSpeed: How quickly do you want to know something moved off-plan?
wizard.largeLoanBase: Below what loan size is it 'not worth a discussion'? (₪)
wizard.applyNote: You can refine any single value later on the registry page — the wizard and manual overrides use the same mechanism.
wizard.nothingChanged: Your answers match the current values — nothing changed.
wizard.institutionDependence: Having most of your money managed by ONE institution bothers you…
wizard.paymentRiseSensitivity: If your mortgage payment crept up over the years, that would be…
wizard.dataStrictness: When some data is missing or stale, advice should…
wizard.taxablePortfolioAge_3: A decade+ — large share is gains
wizard.advicePriority: What matters most when ranking recommendations?

goals.emptyHint: A goal defines where your wealth should go — an emergency fund, retirement, financial independence, or children's education. Add your first goal to see funding gaps and recommendations.
goals.title: Goals
goals.name: Name
goals.gapHint: Verified assets only, consolidated to ILS. Priority-ordered allocation: LIQUID pool for all goals; RETIREMENT pool reserved for retirement goals. Real return assumption

notComputable.TARGET_IN_PAST: Target date is in the past

goals.empty: No goals yet. The strategy engine optimizes against your goals — define them.
goals.incomeModeHint: FI/Retirement goals: capital target derived automatically
goals.guidanceGap: To stay on track: save about {saving} per month toward this goal.
goals.guidanceOnTrack: On track — current assets and projection cover this goal.

strategy.explainer1: Strategy runs pure engines over your verified wealth picture and produces strategy-level recommendations only — never specific products or securities.
strategy.explainer2: Every recommendation carries full reasoning: why, benefits, risks, tax and liquidity implications, time horizon, sensitivity, and alternatives — linked to the evidence and assumptions it relies on.
strategy.explainer3: Accepting or rejecting a recommendation is recorded in the journal. Changing an assumption invalidates recommendations pinned to the old version.
strategy.title: Strategy
strategy.wrongPhase: Strategy is locked until verification is complete. Advance to the Strategy phase on the Verification page.
strategy.ranSummary: Analysis complete: {created} recommendations proposed ({superseded} previous superseded).
strategy.dataGap: The engine refused to produce recommendations — data quality is insufficient:
strategy.empty: No recommendations yet. Run the analysis.

risk.title: Risk questionnaire (drives the allocation target)
risk.hint: Three answers derive the household's target growth share. Changing them re-versions the assumptions and invalidates pinned recommendations.
risk.lossTolerance_1: Low — losses keep me up at night
risk.lossTolerance_2: Medium — uncomfortable but I stay the course
risk.saved: Answers saved — the target growth share above is updated.
risk.drawdownReaction_1: I would sell to stop the bleeding
risk.drawdownReaction_3: I would invest more at the lows
risk.spendingFlexibility: Could you cut expenses in a bad stretch?

resolve.CLOSE_SURVIVOR_GAP: Mapping → Insurance: add or edit a policy with type = Life for the insured member with adequate coverage, then re-run strategy.
resolve.ADD_DISABILITY_COVER: Mapping → Insurance: add a policy with type = Disability for the earner, then re-run strategy.
resolve.CLOSE_MORTGAGE_LIFE_GAP: Mapping → edit the mortgage policy: set type = Mortgage life with coverage ≥ the outstanding balance, then re-run strategy. A Property-type policy is not counted as life cover.
resolve.MAXIMIZE_HISHTALMUT_HEADROOM: Mapping → Cash flow: add a Study-fund contribution flow for the member with the amount, then re-run strategy.
resolve.MAXIMIZE_PENSION_HEADROOM: Mapping → Cash flow: add a Pension contribution flow for the member with the amount, then re-run strategy.

journal.explainer1: The journal records the lifecycle of every decision: what you accepted or rejected, the expected outcome, and when it was planned for implementation.
journal.explainer2: When something actually happens you can record the real outcome against the expectation — closing the loop between decision and reality.
journal.explainer3: Recording outcomes is a monitoring activity and is available at any phase of the process.
journal.title: Decision journal
journal.hint: The household's institutional memory: every decision, the expectation behind it, and what actually happened.
journal.empty: No decisions recorded yet. Accept or reject recommendations on the Strategy page.

scenarios.title: Scenarios
scenarios.hint: Deterministic what-if projections in today's purchasing power (real terms), against the same verified snapshot. Locked to the Strategy phase.
scenarios.wrongPhase: Scenarios are locked until the Strategy phase.
scenarios.explainer1: Scenarios answer "what if" questions: pick a situation (saving more, losing a job, refinancing) and a horizon, and the engine projects your net worth year by year in TODAY’s purchasing power — once as things are (baseline) and once with the scenario applied.
scenarios.explainer2: Reading the table: each column is projected net worth at that year; the Δ row is the scenario’s effect. "Lowest point of liquid capital" is the worst moment for accessible money; "capital depleted" warns if you would run out entirely ("never" is good).
scenarios.explainer3: "Goal outcomes" shows whether each goal ends up funded under each path. Nothing here changes your data — scenarios are safe experiments, saved for comparison.
scenarios.taxNote: Projections apply tax-aware withdrawals: drawdowns are pulled taxable → study-fund → pension, taxed per the registry.
scenarios.beyondHorizon: Beyond the simulated horizon — raise the years

monitoring.explainer1: Monitoring compares your current wealth picture to the baseline set during strategy and flags drift — in net worth, liquidity, concentration, allocation, or goal coverage — plus valuations that have gone stale.
monitoring.explainer2: Each alert carries a severity and a recommended action (re-verify or re-run strategy). You can acknowledge an alert or act on it.
monitoring.explainer3: A cycle runs automatically each day, and you can also run one manually. Acting on an alert resolves it and records the round.
monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.hint: Phase 4 — scheduled snapshots watch for drift against your strategy and stale valuations. Re-evaluate to close the loop.
monitoring.wrongPhase: Monitoring is the fourth phase. Reach it from Strategy once recommendations are in place; the timeline below stays visible in every phase.
monitoring.noAlerts: No open alerts — your household is tracking to strategy.
monitoring.noRuns: No monitoring runs yet. Run one now or wait for the scheduled worker.
monitoring.reevaluateHint: Route the household back to close the loop:
monitoring.noBaseline: No strategy baseline yet — drift is measured once a strategy has run.

journey.MAPPING: Now: map every account, property, mortgage, flow and policy. When the picture is complete — move to verification.
journey.VERIFICATION: Now: confirm each item's value and resolve suspense. When everything is verified — the gate to strategy opens.
journey.STRATEGY: Now: answer the risk questionnaire, set goals, run the engine, accept/reject recommendations — then move to monitoring.
journey.MONITORING: Now: the system watches drift daily. Handle alerts; re-enter strategy when something material changes.
journey.ALLOCATION: Now: review the deployment plan for free cash — approve it (or adjust and rebuild) to open the strategy gate.

allocation.title: Allocation — deploy your free money
allocation.intro: The engine reviews your liquid assets, protects the emergency buffer, and orders every free shekel: expensive debt first (a guaranteed return), then unused tax ceilings, then investing toward your target mix. Approve the plan to unlock the strategy phase.
allocation.wrongPhase: The household is not in the allocation phase. The plan is built after verification is complete.
allocation.expensesUnknown: Monthly expenses are not mapped, so the engine refuses to declare any cash 'free'. Map your expense flows in Mapping and rebuild.
allocation.noSteps: No deployment steps — the buffer consumes all available cash.
allocation.approvedNext: Plan approved — the gate to the strategy phase is open (verification page → advance).
allocation.introV2: The engine computes THREE deployment philosophies for your free cash. Compare their numbers, advantages, and risks; choose one; then approve or decline each step individually. The gate to strategy opens when every step is decided.
allocation.legacyPlan: This plan predates the variant engine — rebuild to get the three-path comparison.

GROWTH.name: Growth path
GROWTH.tagline: Invest everything; the mortgage stays untouched.

DEBT_FREE.name: Debt-reduction path
DEBT_FREE.tagline: Repay mortgage tracks first; certainty over expectation.

BALANCED.name: Balanced path
BALANCED.tagline: Math decides: expensive debt repaid, the rest invested.

allocation.chooseFirst: Choose a path above to start deciding its steps.
allocation.stepsHint: Approve or decline each step. Declined steps stay in the audit trail. All steps decided = plan approved.
